validation.concept: Columnar Parquet encoding (dictionary, RLE, bit-packing) vs row CSV
validation.proof: CSV=597.7MB vs Parquet/Snappy=171.6MB (3.48x smaller); Parquet/Zstd=89.7MB (6.66x smaller); Scan speedup=7.24x (less I/O beats decompression overhead)
validation.validates: Article claim: columnar storage encodes repetitive string columns (region, category) with dictionary encoding achieving high compression; smaller file = fewer disk reads = faster scans
validation.interpretation: Parquet (Snappy) is 3.48x smaller than CSV because columnar layout groups identical values (region has only 5 distinct values across 5977M rows), enabling dictionary encoding and RLE. Scanning Parquet is 7.24x faster because fewer bytes cross the I/O bus — decompression CPU cost is negligible compared to disk/memory bandwidth savings. This is the core L03 insight: 'access patterns should match the storage layout.'
